skill.name: ckm-banner-design
skill.description: Design social, advertising, website, and print banners with platform size guidance, art direction options, AI visual prompts, and export steps.
skill.summary: Create platform-ready banners with size rules, style options, generated visuals, and export guidance.
skill.author: nextlevelbuilder

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but the weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are false positives from banner dimensions, design terms, and markdown examples. Confirmed concerns are documented local command examples, external Pinterest research, localhost screenshot URLs, and generated asset writes. No evidence found of prompt injection, credential access, malicious exfiltration, or obfuscated code.

medium_findings.title: Documented Local Command Execution
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for command-use risk. The skill instructs agents to run local Python and Node scripts for prompt search, image generation, and screenshot export. The commands appear task-related and not malicious, but they execute local tooling and use user-provided prompt and size values.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks are explicit bash examples that run local scripts. The surrounding context is legitimate banner generation and export, with no evidence of destructive or secret-stealing behavior.

medium_findings.title: External Research and Browser Network Use
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for network exposure. The workflow directs browser research on Pinterest and uses localhost URLs for screenshot export. This is consistent with design research and local rendering, but it should be disclosed because it can contact external sites.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Pinterest workflow clearly requires external browsing, while localhost URLs are local export endpoints. There is no evidence that secrets or user data are sent to an unauthorized endpoint.

medium_findings.title: Local File Reads and Generated Asset Writes
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for filesystem activity. The skill checks a brand guideline file, reads scripts under .claude skill directories, and writes banner outputs under assets/banners. This is normal for the workflow, but it requires workspace file access.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file paths are directly visible and align with local asset production. I did not find evidence of reading sensitive files or writing outside the project asset paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The flagged lines contain banner dimensions, style names, model labels, markdown headings, and design terms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, weak cipher use, hashing APIs, or cryptographic logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found design copy and markdown content only. There are no cryptographic functions or weak algorithm references in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The cited lines describe visual layout, safe zones, dark mode styling, and no-text image prompts. They do not enumerate users, hosts, processes, environment variables, or system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The scanner appears to have matched isolated words in design guidance. The surrounding context is banner composition, not host or account reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Alerts Are Mostly False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for most reported backtick locations. Inline markdown paths, skill names, aspect ratios, output conventions, and search query templates were flagged as Ruby backtick execution. Only the fenced bash examples are real command examples and are covered in medium findings.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is markdown documentation rather than Ruby code. The few actual command examples are separated into confirmed medium-risk findings.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Script Execution Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes bash examples that call Python and Node scripts. These should run only from trusted skill directories and with reviewed prompt, path, and dimension inputs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and task-related. Risk depends on the integrity of the referenced local scripts and inputs.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Browser Research
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow directs agents to browse Pinterest for design references. This is not malicious, but it is external network activity.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The browser research instruction is clear and intentional. No credential or private data transmission was found.

content.value_statement: Banner sizing and art direction can be slow across platforms. This skill guides banner concepts, AI visuals, layout rules, and export steps.
actual_capabilities: Collects banner purpose, platform, content, brand, style, and quantity requirements.
Maps common platforms to recommended banner sizes and safe zones.
Suggests multiple art direction styles for social, ad, web, and print banners.
Guides HTML and CSS banner composition with typography, CTA, and contrast rules.
Provides AI visual generation prompts and local export steps for PNG banners.
Organizes exported variants with campaign folders and size-based filenames.
limitations: Requires other local skills and scripts for image generation, browser screenshots, and visual display.
May use external browsing for Pinterest reference research when enabled by the user.
Does not handle video editing, full website design, or final print production.
Print guidance is conceptual and does not replace printer-specific proofing.

use_cases.title: Launch Campaign Banners
use_cases.description: Create several banner directions for a product launch across LinkedIn, Google Display, and website hero placements.

use_cases.title: Creator Channel Headers
use_cases.description: Design YouTube, Twitter/X, and Facebook headers with correct dimensions, safe zones, and consistent visual style.

use_cases.title: Event Promotion Assets
use_cases.description: Generate social, web, and print banner concepts for an event with clear CTA placement and brand alignment.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Social Banner
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a LinkedIn banner for my consulting business. Use a clean style, include the headline, and suggest safe placement for my logo.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Platform Campaign
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for related banner variants.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design three banner options for a product launch across Instagram post, Twitter/X header, and website hero. Include dimensions and art direction notes.

prompt_templates.title: Branded Ad Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with brand and conversion goals.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Google Display ad banners for a SaaS trial campaign. Use our brand colors, one CTA, strong contrast, and variants for 300x250 and 728x90.

prompt_templates.title: Full Creative System
prompt_templates.prompt: Develop a banner system for an event campaign. Provide three art directions, AI visual prompts, safe zones, export names, and iteration notes.

output_examples.input: Design a Twitter/X header for a fintech newsletter.
output_examples.output: Three concepts: minimalist trust-focused layout, bold typography announcement style, and editorial grid style. Each includes 1500x500 sizing, safe zone notes, headline placement, and CTA guidance.

output_examples.input: Create Google ad banners for a spring sale.
output: 300x250 medium rectangle with product photo direction.
728x90 leaderboard with short headline and single CTA.
320x100 mobile banner with simplified copy and high contrast.

output_examples.input: Prepare a website hero banner for a conference.
output_examples.output: A 1920px wide hero direction with speaker imagery, large headline, registration CTA, brand color accents, and export naming for campaign assets.

best_practices: Provide final headline, CTA, platform, dimensions, and brand rules before generation begins.
Keep key text inside safe zones and use one clear CTA per banner.
Generate text-free visuals first, then overlay copy in HTML or design tooling.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for one banner to fit every platform without resizing and safe zone checks.
Do not place critical text near edges or under expected platform interface areas.
Do not rely on AI-generated text inside images for final banner copy.

faq.question: Which banner platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: It covers common social covers, Google Display sizes, website heroes, email headers, and basic print banner formats.

faq.question: Can it create multiple design options?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow asks for a quantity and recommends several art direction options for review.

faq.question: Does it generate final image files?
faq.answer: It provides local generation and screenshot export steps. Actual export depends on the referenced local skills and scripts.

faq.question: Can it follow brand guidelines?
faq.answer: Yes, when brand colors, typography, logos, and message rules are available in the workspace or user prompt.

faq.question: Is it suitable for print production?
faq.answer: It includes print sizing, DPI, bleed, and color mode guidance, but final files still need printer proofing.

faq.question: Does it use external websites?
faq.answer: It may use Pinterest for reference research if browser access is enabled and the user allows that workflow.
